title: Blinded gold adjudication receipt
description: One human reviewer's decision over one gold candidate file, content-bound to the exact candidate bytes — the same proposal/decision separation the memory ledger enforces, applied to test data. Two accept receipts from verifiably distinct reviewers are required before freeze-pack.mjs will freeze a clip. v1 enforces distinct declared identities (name AND git identity) and reviewer != drafter; anchoring each receipt in the reviewer's own git commit ancestry is the documented expectation but is not yet mechanically verified — see bench/README.md.

review_id.description: Content id of this receipt's canonical body (everything except review_id). A receipt whose id does not match its bytes is invalid.

candidate.description: File receipt of the exact gold candidate bytes reviewed. A decision that does not bind current bytes cannot be used to freeze — same rule as the memory ledger.

reviewer.description: Named human identity. Must differ from the drafter and from the other freezing reviewer on BOTH fields. String inequality alone cannot prove two humans exist; recruiting a real second Korean-fluent reviewer is the binding constraint on this whole pipeline and is not solvable in code.

drafter.description: Copied from the candidate so the receipt is self-describing; must match the candidate bytes.

blinded.description: True only if the reviewer saw source media plus candidate gold without seeing any system's output for these windows. Freeze requires true.

minutes_spent.description: Honest per-unit cost accounting. The claim that agent drafting cuts adjudication cost must be measured, not asserted.
